definitions: A type of linear regression that uses shrinkage to improve prediction accuracy and interpretability by penalizing the absolute size of regression coefficients.
A regularization method that performs both variable selection and regularization in order to enhance the prediction accuracy and interpretability of the statistical model it produces.
examples: The researcher applied LASSO regression to identify the most significant predictors.
LASSO regression is useful when dealing with high-dimensional data.
synonyms: least absolute shrinkage and selection operator
